decks.notes: This deck focuses on playing aggressively, using cheap spells and creatures to control the tempo of the game. By efficiently countering and removing threats while applying pressure with evasive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and win quickly. The inclusion of cards like Daze and Force of Will allows for flexibility in protecting key threats and disrupting the opponent's plays. With a balanced mix of disruption, card selection, and threats, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then use spells like Reanimate, Shallow Grave, and Animate Dead to bring them back to the battlefield for a big swing. The deck includes disruptive elements like Unmask, Thoughtseize, and Cabal Therapy to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With efficient mana acceleration from cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

This deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly using fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Lion's Eye Diamond, along with rituals like Dark Ritual. The goal is to cast multiple spells in a single turn to build up storm count, enabling a powerful finisher like Tendrils of Agony to drain the opponent's life total. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Hexing Squelcher and Veil of Summer to protect the combo. Burning Wish allows for additional flexibility in finding the right card for the situation. Overall, the deck aims to create a fast and consistent storm combo to win the game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty using spells like Reanimate, Shallow Grave, and Animate Dead. The deck also utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Unmask to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect the combo pieces. With the use of Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to accelerate its game plan and win the game quickly before the opponent can react. Additionally, cards like Faithless Looting and Collective Brutality provide card advantage and further disruption to control the game.

The main idea of this deck is to use Painter's Servant to change all the cards in the opponent's library to a chosen color, then use Grindstone to mill them out quickly. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps to recur artifacts from the graveyard, while Pinnacle Emissary and Kappa Cannoneer provide additional utility. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Force of Will and Sink into Stupor, as well as ramp in the form of Lotus Petal and Mox Opal. The overall strategy is to quickly assemble the combo and win the game before the opponent can react.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back into play with reanimation spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead. The goal is to cheat out big threats like Archon of Cruelty and Atraxa, Grand Unifier early in the game to overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards like Faithless Looting and Cabal Therapy to help fill the graveyard and disrupt the opponent's strategy. With a combination of fast mana generation and graveyard recursion, this deck aims to win the game through powerful combo plays.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to control the game and lock down the opponent's options. By utilizing powerful artifacts like Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up its own resources with Urza's Saga and Urza's Workshop. The inclusion of cards like Grim Monolith and Candelabra of Tawnos helps ramp up mana quickly to play game-ending threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The deck's main goal is to establish a strong board presence through artifact control and stifle the opponent's ability to play effectively.

The key concept of this deck is to play aggressively with a focus on utilizing cards with low mana cost and tempo advantage. By quickly casting efficient spells and disrupting the opponent with countermagic and land destruction, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and overwhelm the opponent with threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent. The inclusion of card draw and filtering tools like Brainstorm and Ponder helps to maintain card advantage and consistency in executing the game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly deploy artifact creatures and utilize the powerful synergy between artifacts and various spells to overwhelm the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures like Pinnacle Emissary and Memory Guardian with disruptive spells like Sink into Stupor and Force of Will, as well as valuable artifacts like Urza's Saga and Mox Opal, the deck aims to establish board presence and control the game tempo. Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides card advantage and recursion options, while Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Bauble offer additional utility. The inclusion of mana acceleration sources like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb enable rapid deployment of threats and disruption.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack to quickly put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield. These creatures can end the game in just a few turns, overwhelming the opponent with their sheer power. The deck also includes various card draw and manipulation spells to help find the combo pieces quickly, as well as protection in the form of Force of Will and other counterspells. The deck aims to win the game before the opponent has a chance to react.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to create a prison-like control strategy, locking down opponents' resources and limiting their options. By utilizing powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and ultimately overpower them with threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The combination of mana acceleration from Grim Monolith and Urza's lands, as well as card advantage from Urza's Saga, allows the deck to establish a strong board presence and control the game.

The deck focuses on creating a lockdown situation for the opponent by utilizing artifacts to control the game. The key strategy involves using cards like Trinisphere and The One Ring to restrict the opponent's options and disrupt their gameplay. Cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms provide additional control elements, while Urza's lands and Grim Monolith help generate the mana needed to deploy powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. Overall, the deck aims to establish dominance through a combination of artifacts and control elements.

This deck aims to control the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and disrupting their game plan while setting up its own win condition. It focuses on generating value through card draw and mana denial, ultimately leading to a powerful board presence with cards like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Teferi, Time Raveler helps to control the tempo of the game, while cards like Stifle and Spell Snare provide additional disruption. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and includes key lands like Wasteland and Karakas.

This deck focuses on using efficient and aggressive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent to pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The deck also aims to maintain tempo with cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to find the right cards for the situation. Wasteland and Cori-Steel Cutter can disrupt the opponent's mana base, while Lightning Bolt provides reach to close out games. The deck's main goal is to maintain board presence, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and close out the game quickly.

The main concept of this Doomsday combo deck is to efficiently and quickly assemble the necessary pieces to win the game by using Doomsday to manipulate the top of the library and win with Thassa's Oracle. The deck focuses on control elements such as counterspells and disruption spells like Thoughtseize to protect the combo while digging through the deck with cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder. The deck aims to set up a winning turn by generating enough mana with rituals and Lion's Eye Diamond, while also ensuring a clear path for victory with Thassa's Oracle.

The main concept of this deck is to establish early aggression through efficient spellcasting and creature presence, utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Lightning Bolt, and Delver of Secrets. It aims to control the tempo of the game by countering threats with Force of Will and Daze, while also providing card advantage and manipulation through Ponder and Mishra's Bauble. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Murktide Regent and Dragon's Rage Channeler to close out the game quickly. Additionally, the deck includes disruption in the form of Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base.
